beats.prompt: Match each result to its value — E[pattern] means the expected (average) wait for that pattern. Tap a result on the left, then its match on the right.

feedback.correct: All matched — those are the headline numbers of the course.
hints: Tap a result, then its value.
HH waited longer than HT; THH is longer still.

beats.prompt: Read why mixed practice works, then continue.

interaction.title: Mixed beats blocked
interaction.body: Practicing different kinds of problems together feels harder than drilling one kind — and that's exactly why it works better. You're learning to tell them apart, the way a real problem arrives without a label.

beats.prompt: Match each pattern to its average wait. Which one waits the longest?

feedback.correct: Right — HTH waits 10, the longest here. Structure, not length, sets the wait.

beats.prompt: Two friends watch ONE coin: one waits for HH, the other for HT. Press Run to watch one race, then run the batch — who appears first more often?

hero.structuralReadout: On one shared stream, HH and HT each win about 50 of every 100 races — a dead tie.

feedback.correct: A tie — "who's first" on a shared stream isn't "who's faster alone."
hints: This is a race, not a solo wait.

beats.prompt: Match each kind of recurrence — the rule linking a state to its neighbors — to its per-flip cost.

feedback.correct: Right — durations pay 1 per flip; a probability has no per-flip cost.
hints: One counts flips, one splits chances.

beats.prompt: On the HH machine: you've matched a lone H (state E1). Tap where each next flip sends you.

feedback.correct: Reset — and that extra restart is exactly why HH (6) beats HT (4).
hints: A T breaks the HH streak.

beats.prompt: Reveal the recap cards to see how wait, race, and walk all connect.
beats.interviewNote: Mixed retrieval like this is the spaced-practice core of durable problem-solving: when the problem arrives unlabeled, you've rehearsed choosing the right tool (wait vs race, probability vs duration).

feedback.correct: Wait, race, walk — you can now tell them apart on sight.
hints: Pick the right tool per problem.
